facts.text: Montrose hosts the Cortlandt station, a Hudson Metro-North stop that sits right inside the hamlet’s limits.
During the Revolutionary War, Kings Ferry in Montrose was the southernmost Continental Army crossing of the Hudson before moving to Fort Lafayette.
The Hendrick Hudson Free Library, opened in 1931 on the former Kennedy House, sits at 185 Kings Ferry Road and serves Montrose and nearby communities.
Montrose’s name honors the Montross family, many of whom worked at the nearby Buchanan tannery during the 19th century.
The hamlet lacks its own local government, operating instead under the jurisdiction of the Town of Cortlandt.
The Hendrick Hudson Free Library’s original building was the Kennedy House, a historic home that dates back to the early 1900s.
